mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 305.0, inflation expectations trigger immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities toward real assets, producing negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in 65% of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 305.0, or the signal loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous low unemployment and accelerating payroll employment signals robust consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive return in more than 55% of triggered months, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the industrial production index during mid-cycle expansions reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, generating positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in 60% of observations during mid-cycle expansions, or the rolling Rank IC drops below 0.02 over any 24-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate expanding residential construction activity and rising household wealth effects, which directly boost consumer spending and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal yields negative forward returns in 60% of months, or the predictive correlation between HOUST and 20-day equity returns falls below 0.05 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: When real savings exceed 650,000 million dollars, abundant capital availability lowers corporate borrowing costs and funds expansion projects, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in 65% of periods where RSAFS exceeds the threshold, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for monetary policy variables.

mechanism_narrative: Strong real GDP growth combined with stable consumer prices creates a favorable macroeconomic environment that supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to improve model fit (F-test p > 0.05), or forward returns are negative in 60% of observations when both conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating payroll employment growth during late-cycle expansions signals weakening labor demand and impending economic slowdown, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in 65% of late-cycle observations with payroll deceleration, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward testing.
